subject: Sociology
title: Why Society Needs Schools
description: Learn Why Society Needs Schools in one minute.

hook.text: Before schools existed, children learned by watching adults. Now we lock them in rooms for twelve years.

buildup.text: Formal education arose because industrial societies needed literate, disciplined workers at massive scale.

discovery.text: Schools don't just teach skills. They socialize children into shared values, norms, and national identity.

twist.text: Compulsory education was controversial. Many families saw it as the state taking control of their children.

climax.text: Education is society's main tool for reproducing itself — passing culture from one generation to the next.

punchline.text: Schools teach society, not just subjects.

quiz.question: What is a key social function of schools beyond teaching skills?
options: Socializing children into shared values and norms
